reconcile_reason: Status refreshed after current-branch Phase-2 GUI/runtime proof, Integration Validator/spec-owner dispositions, and post-7f055da6 VM/swarm hardening commits.
method: Adversarial per-subscope re-verification against CURRENT working-tree code (HEAD on feat branch after the IV's f0c6faf0 backup snapshot). One Explore sub-reviewer per subscope cluster; each defaulted a blocker to OPEN unless current code proved it CLOSED with file:line evidence. Two IV linchpins re-verified directly + one decisive runtime proof (real TinyLlama candle load).
decisive_finding: The IV Phase-2 FAIL verdict was rendered against backup snapshot f0c6faf0 (2026-05-30T01:00). A large batch of remediation landed in the working tree AFTER that snapshot (e.g. MT-202 production model-load wiring at 2026-05-30T11:19, MT-029 inspector read-auth at 23:55, sandbox network-honesty at 22:42, SSM suppress_next_reset at 18:29/19:59, MT-097/100/102 server-side gates at 13:11). Both IV-verified linchpins are CLOSED in current code; 22 of 30 confirmed blockers are CLOSED with evidence; 8 remain OPEN. The verdict is STALE, not wrong-at-the-time.

L1-steering-not-wired.evidence: app/src-tauri/src/commands/model_runtime.rs:362-419 kernel_model_runtime_load is a real #[tauri::command] that constructs a real CandleRuntime via load_local_candle_model and calls state.attach_live_runtime(model_id, Arc::new(runtime)) at :416. Registered in app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs invoke_handler (both macro arms, :73-74 and :179-180). ModelRuntimeState managed at lib.rs:882. All four IPC families (steering/refusal/caa/lora) resolve state.live_runtime via require_live_runtime and dispatch into real technique functions (steering.rs:270/306/378/452/468, refusal.rs:79, caa.rs:85, lora.rs:175/204/221/243).
L1-steering-not-wired.runtime_proof: model_load_attaches_real_candle_runtime_and_capture_returns_real_activations executed against real Maykeye_TinyLLama-v0 model.safetensors (sha256 c302246d...) — see proof_runs.

L2-loopback-deny.evidence: podman_cli.rs:276 NetPolicy::LoopbackOnly => Ok(PODMAN_LOOPBACK_NETWORK_MODE) (slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns,allow_host_loopback=true), NOT 'none'. kernel_003_bridge.rs:264 NetPolicy::LoopbackOnly => Err(docker_loopback_unsupported()) — loud typed failure, no silent downgrade.

MT-095.1.evidence: Commit c3eb3f9f: KvCacheHandle::prefix_restore now enforces enforce_prefix_ttl(handle, ops.prefix_cache_ttl_seconds()) with a typed expiry error before dispatch; KvCacheOps gains a defaulted prefix_cache_ttl_seconds() (0=no expiry) overridden by LlamaCppKvCache from its policy TTL; TTL measured from the handle's bound registered_at_utc_micros. Test kv_security_facade_restore_enforces_prefix_cache_ttl proves 'wait past TTL -> Err' + fresh passes (default CI, no sleep).

MT-095.2.evidence: Commit c3eb3f9f: KvCacheHandle::prefix_restore(model_id, handle) is the SINGLE restore chokepoint every caller funnels through (kv_cache_technique + subquadratic thread model_id); it calls verify_self_against before dispatch, closing the in-crate facade bypass. subquadratic::state_commit now binds (mirrors kv_cache_technique), closing a previously-ungated subquadratic restore. Commit f65c1bce (adversarial-review fix): load_into_handle (MT-117 cross-session) now binds the re-minted handle so cross-session state recovery survives the gate. Tests: kv_security 15 (4 new facade/TTL), inf9_cross_session 21 (regression guard), subquadratic 8 — candle + default.

MT-118.evidence: Finding discovered during MT-095 testing (not an IV-listed blocker; pre-existing). mt_118_all_ssm_architectures_declare_full_parity_capabilities asserted SSM supports_activation_steering=true, but commit 1018e034 deliberately set it false (SSM steering_hooks() -> bare CandleSteeringHooks capture() fails closed; true was a lie) per the MT-116 deferral and updated 3 sibling candle suites, missing this parity test. Commit e7c4bf30 aligned the stale assertion to the honest false (apply-primitive parity proven separately). All 4 SSM capability suites green. FOLLOW-UP FEATURE (deferred, MT-116): wire SSM real-forward steering capture so the capability can honestly become true; spec 4.7.1c text drift needs a deferral note (spec-writer task).

MT-186.a.evidence: Integration Validator disposition receipt KERNEL004-MT186A-DISPOSITION-REVIEW-20260601 closes this for WP-KERNEL-004: forced-cancel reclaim primitive is implemented and proven; no production MicroTaskExecutor instantiation exists in current scope, so production wiring is future-WP territory and that future WP must construct a reclaim-enabled canceller via MtCanceller::with_reclaim.
MT-186.a.fix: No WP-KERNEL-004 production wiring remains available without fabricating a production executor. Keep future production MicroTaskExecutor creation gated on reclaim-enabled cancellation.
